title: BUbuisnessAndMarket
description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from BUbuisnessAndMarket, skipping any feeds with errors.

items.title: OpenAI’s Codex and Anthropic’s Claude spark coding revolution as developers say they’ve abandoned traditional programming
items.description: New releases from OpenAI and Anthropic sparked an existential crisis among coders, but many engineers say they stopped coding months ago.

items.title: How leaders are protecting culture while AI rewrites how work gets done
items.description: At Ingka Group, the largest IKEA retailer in 32 countries, leaders set out to adopt AI in a way that wouldn’t put their culture at risk.

items.title: You can now use buy-now-pay-later to pay your rent — but watch out for the fees
Tax refunds are up this year, early IRS numbers show. See how yours compares.
Who needs chocolates? Vacuums and car tires are the new Valentine's Day treats.
Trump says regime change in Iran that ousts Islamic clerics ‘would be the best thing that could happen’ as another carrier heads to Mideast
items.description: Trump said the USS Gerald R. Ford, the world's largest aircraft carrier, is being sent from the Caribbean Sea to the Mideast.

items.title: Bank of America lifts Moynihan’s pay 17% to $41 million for 2025
items.description: A year ago, Moynihan’s compensation was increased 21% to $35 million after the bank was able to boost its earnings.

items.title: Marc Andreessen made a dire software prediction 15 years ago. Now it’s happening in a way nobody imagined
items.description: The billionaire venture capitalist explained why software would eat the world in 2011. He had no idea.

items.title: Despite cooling inflation, gas and electricity bills are still painfully costly
Amazon’s stock just clinched its worst losing streak in nearly 20 years
